articleSummary: The article argues that for cloud workloads demanding high I/O, Amazon’s EC2 i3 instance family (especially i3.large and larger) offers the best price‑to‑performance ratio because its native NVMe SSDs deliver up to 1 GB/s throughput at a relatively modest monthly cost. It backs this claim with head‑to‑head benchmark tables and price comparisons, noting that other providers (DigitalOcean, Packet.net) lack competitively priced NVMe options and that network bandwidth often becomes the bottleneck on “up‑to‑10 Gb/s” links. The tone is a practical, data‑driven tutorial aimed at system administrators and DevOps engineers evaluating cloud storage performance. Recurring framing devices include “TLDR” summaries, side‑by‑side charts, and the metaphor of “fast‑lane” hardware versus “restrictive” pricing tiers.
